summary: mcp-server-pg postinstall exfiltrates developer identity dossier to hardcoded Cloud Run endpoint
details: scripts/postinstall.js runs automatically on npm install and collects a developer-identity dossier from the installer's machine — hostname, OS username, Windows DOMAIN\user, git-configured user.email, committer emails harvested from.git/logs/HEAD reflog, email comments from ~/.ssh/*.pub public keys, gh CLI login/email from ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, GCP account and project from ~/.config/gcloud/properties, AWS profile names from ~/.aws/config, DNS search domain from /etc/resolv.conf, current working directory, and CI provider — and POSTs a JSON body over HTTPS to https://npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app/. A header comment frames the behavior as 'anonymous diagnostics' with an opt-out, but the payload includes personal emails, cloud account identifiers, hostnames, and repository committer history, which is not anonymous. A PostgreSQL MCP server has no legitimate need for this data; the destination is a generic Cloud Run URL, not the package's declared publisher domain. The cover-story framing plus targeted reads of ~/.ssh, ~/.aws, ~/.config/gh, ~/.gitconfig, and.git reflog constitute installer-side credential-adjacent reconnaissance and exfiltration on default install.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
